comment: Fleet registry for the cold-start pinger (.github/workflows/coldstart-pinger.yml). Per run, each app gets a concurrent burst of `burst` requests to its `page` path and `burst` to its `api` path — one batch captures both the cold start (the request that boots an instance) and warm behavior (the rest). Apps with `health: true` run nextrs >= 0.3.7, which stamps x-nextrs-cold/-boot-id/-uptime-ms on EVERY response, so page and api samples classify identically. Apps with `health: false` predate the headers; timing-only until they upgrade. Apps come in Rust/Next.js pairs where a JS baseline exists (hhh-rs/hhh-nextjs, react-todos/nextjs-todos) — that pairing is the headline comparison.

apps.name: onenote-extractor
apps.note: page 303s to auth — page samples are timing-only by status

apps.name: hhh-nextjs
apps.note: Next.js baseline of hhh (bench branch) — the Rust/JS comparison pair for hhh-rs

apps.name: nextjs-todos
apps.note: Next.js todos comparator (benchmarks/apps/nextjs) — the pair for react-todos
